commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=07a971b65ff88d66957ab337df64f61c7d999a70)
commands.review_summary:
The PR splits exec outcome hashing so completed exec output remains hashed while failed exec outcomes hash only status, exitCode, and timedOut, with a regression test expecting global breaker escalation for varying failed output.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +2. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main hashes failed exec output into resultHash while getNoProgressStreak stops on hash drift; I did not run a live replay because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the contributor proof is mock-only and the availability tradeoff around the failed-exec fingerprint needs maintainer acceptance before merge.

target.title: fix: strip volatile output from exec result hash to fix no-progress detection (#93917)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where repeated failed `exec` calls would never trigger the
no-progress circuit breaker when the error output text varied across calls.
This happens commonly with SSH timeouts, Docker connection-refused errors, and
other transient failures where timestamps or session ids in the error message
change each time while the actual failure mode is identical.

Fixes #93917

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts:213-230` | Split `hashExecToolOutcome` so `completed` keeps output in hash, `failed` excludes volatile output text — only `{status, exitCode, timedOut}` is hashed |
| `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts:639-700` | Restore `date` (completed) test to `warning` level; upgrade `flaky-helper` (failed) test to `critical`/`global_circuit_breaker` |

## Why This Change Was Made

Split `hashExecToolOutcome` so `failed` exec results are hashed without the
volatile output text, while `completed` exec results keep output in the hash
as a real progress signal. A failed command producing different error strings
across identical retries should still count as no-progress.

## User Impact

Stuck agents that loop retrying the same failed `exec` call now correctly hit
the global circuit breaker after the configured threshold, stopping runaway
sessions instead of burning tokens forever.

## Evidence

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ keeps changing exec output below the global no-progress breaker
 ✓ escalates repeated failed exec calls to the global breaker when status and exitCode are stable
 ✓ blocks repeated completed exec calls despite volatile runtime details
 ✓ blocks repeated running exec calls despite volatile session details and text
 ...
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  59 passed (59)
```

### Scenario walkthrough

**Scenario A — Docker failure loop (before fix → after fix):**
```
before: 30 "docker ps" calls, each "connection refused (attempt N)"
        → noProgressStreak always 1 (error text varies by attempt number)
        → agent burns tokens forever, never hits circuit breaker

after:  30 "docker ps" calls, each "connection refused (attempt N)"
        → noProgressStreak reaches 30 ({status:failed, exitCode:1, timedOut:false} stable)
        → global_circuit_breaker triggers, stops runaway session
```

**Scenario B — `date` in a loop (before fix → after fix, unchanged):**
```
before: 30 "date" calls, each returns different timestamp
        → noProgressStreak stays at 1 (completed output varies as expected)
        → stuck at warning level only — output IS real progress

after:  same behavior — completed output intentionally kept in hash,
        varying completed output remains a real progress signal
```

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: sunlit-deng
